reasoning: The threshold requires >10 fatalities in the 30 days preceding each resolution date. Historic data shows this level of violence in Bolivia only occurs during severe political crises (such as late 2019). The 2025 elections have already successfully concluded, with Rodrigo Paz winning decisively in the October 19 runoff and taking office peacefully on November 8. With the political situation stabilized and the former ruling MAS party fractured, the likelihood of major violent protests has drastically decreased. There are no reports of significant unrest or fatalities in November or December 2025. Therefore, the probability for the closest date (Dec 28) is effectively at the operational minimum (0.05), with only slight increases for January and March 2026 to reflect normal uncertainty over longer horizons.
